post.author: Story Makers
post.caption: 9.3K104103Story Makers · FollowStory Makers · Original audioThe boy broke through the mourners covered in mud and rain.His wet gray shirt clung to his thin body, and his face was streaked with tears as he ran straight toward the white coffin covered in lilies.“Please, sir, don’t bury your daughter!”The grieving father turned, stunned.Security reached for the boy, but the father grabbed him first by the shoulders.“What are you saying?”The boy could barely breathe.The rain hit his face as he pointed at the coffin.“She’s not dead. She’s only in a coma!”The mourners gasped.The father’s hands tightened.“Don’t say that.”The boy shook his head, crying harder.“I heard her breathe. I saw her move.”Behind them, the stepmother in black went still beneath her umbrella.The boy turned and pointed at her.“Your wife gave her something to drink.”The father’s face twisted in pain.“My wife would never hurt her.”But his voice broke.The boy stepped closer through the mud, eyes desperate.“Sir, look me in the eye. She’s still alive.”The father slowly turned toward the coffin.👉 Part 2 in the comments See less ::-webkit-scrollbar { -webkit-appearance: none; width: 7px; } ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { border-radius: 4px; background: var(--scroll-thumb); }11K12185764914
